site.title: GrekAI Skills 4 All
site.tagline: A catalog of agent skills, hooks & connectors

items.title: Tutorial — Narrated Playwright Walkthroughs
items.description: Record narrated, themeable product tutorials by driving any web app with Playwright and voicing every step with a neural TTS voice (Jenny by default). One plain-English goal becomes a drift-free MP4 + WebVTT captions plus a light/dark, logo-branded index.html that switches between all your tutorials. Human-gated: approve the scenario before recording, approve the video before publishing. Ships /tutorial-create, /tutorial-update and /tutorial-status, a config-driven theme, and runnable Playwright + render scripts.
items.usage: Set a one-time tutorial.config.json (brand, paths, voice), then say "/tutorial-create show a new user how to create their first record". The skill drafts a scenario for your approval, records it with Playwright, renders a narrated MP4 + captions with edge-tts, shows it for approval, then rebuilds a themeable light/dark index.html switching between every tutorial. /tutorial-update <slug> re-records one; /tutorial-status reports stale/missing. Needs Node 18+, Playwright, ffmpeg and edge-tts on PATH.

items.title: GemelNet Advisor — Israeli Pension Funds
items.description: Query and analyze Israeli provident, study (קרן השתלמות) and investment-provident (גמל להשקעה) funds via the official GemelNet open dataset. Compare funds, rank one against its like-for-like peers, scan a statement into a portfolio checkup, and estimate shekel revenue — public fund-level data only, framed as data-grounded analysis, not financial advice. Ships with a UserPromptSubmit hook that auto-triggers on Hebrew/English fund keywords.
items.usage: Just ask your assistant — e.g. "compare my study fund 9012 with its peers and tell me what I'm paying in fees" — and it drives SKILL.md. Or run the stdlib-only engine directly: python3 scripts/gemelnet.py fund 9012 (also funds --q, compare, rank, revenue). Optionally wire the bundled UserPromptSubmit hook so it auto-triggers on Hebrew/English pension keywords. Public fund-level data only — analysis, not financial advice.

items.title: No-Repo Collaborative Deck
items.description: Serverless, AI-driven presentation collaboration. Stateful HTML reads/writes a sidecar state.json synced over a shared cloud folder; an MCP agent rewrites the deck from approved changes. Figma-style comment pins, AI prompt drafts and approvals — no server, no repo.
items.usage: Open the deck HTML in a Chromium-based browser and pick a synced cloud folder when prompted (File System Access API). Add Figma-style comment pins and draft AI prompts; once a change is approved in state.json, the MCP agent rewrites the deck. Try the live demo first.

items.title: Session Handoff
items.description: Package the current working session so the NEXT session resumes with ZERO re-research. Produces a dated handoff doc, a standalone resume-prompt, and a session-log metrics row. Works in any repo — run it when context is nearly exhausted or you say "hand off".
items.usage: When your session is nearly out of context, say "hand off" (or invoke the skill). It writes a dated handoff doc, a standalone copy-paste resume prompt, and an appended session-log.csv row under the project's docs/handoffs/ folder, then commits them.

items.title: Setup Memory — Memory-in-Repo
items.description: Move Claude's per-project memory INTO the repo (memories/repo/) and auto-load it via a filesystem junction. Memory becomes committed, team-shared, and token-efficient: pay once to write it, save tokens every future session. Generic and publishable — no secrets, no per-user content.
items.usage:
Run the bundled PowerShell script against your repo root:

    & "$HOME\.claude\skills\setup-memory\scripts\Setup-Memory.ps1" -ProjectPath .

It creates memories/repo/ with a MEMORY.md index and junctions it into ~/.claude/projects/<slug>/memory. Idempotent, no admin required (add -WhatIf to preview).

items.title: Dev Rules & 13-Agent Roster
items.description: A portable bundle of general engineering rules (coding, refactoring, working-style) plus a reusable 13-agent specialist roster and a routing standard. Drop it into any codebase to bootstrap CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / .cursor rules and stand up frontend, backend, refactor, QA, security, devops and orchestration subagents.
items.usage: Fold rules/* into your project's CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md and copy agents/*.md into .claude/agents/ (transform to .github/agents/*.agent.md for Copilot). Or point your assistant at a single file: "follow rules/refactoring-rules.md" or "use the refactor-test-guardian agent".

items.title: Daily CV — Automated Job Hunt
items.description: Config-driven, self-learning job-search agent. Reads your profile, validates that roles are actively accepting applications, and generates an ATS-optimised CV + cover letter (DOCX + PDF) from verified facts only — never repeating a company+role. Your real profile stays local and gitignored; only sanitized templates ship here.
items.usage: Run INIT.md once to build your gitignored profile/PROFILE.json (the single source of truth). Then invoke the skill daily: each run finds one live, accepting role and generates an ATS-optimised CV + tailored cover letter (DOCX + PDF) from verified facts only — never repeating a company+role.

items.title: My Claude Toolkit
items.description: A portable, hand-authored Claude Code setup: reusable helper command-scripts (commit-and-PR, verify), hooks, a token/cost usage monitor + dashboard, a sparse-worktree engine, and a sanitized MCP-server inventory. Use to bootstrap or restore your Claude Code setup on a new machine. All secrets stripped to env-var placeholders.
items.usage: See SKILL.md for the reinstall steps. Copy the helper command-scripts (commit-and-PR, verify), hooks, and the token/cost usage monitor into ~/.claude/ or your project's .claude/ to restore or bootstrap your Claude Code setup on a new machine. All secrets are stripped to env-var placeholders.
